name: The Memory Machine
short_description: In this interactive story set in a world where memories are a source of energy, Arthur must help the townspeople reclaim their past. Along the way he will befriend talking vegetables, explore a magical forest, and uncover the town’s mysterious history.
detailed_description:
Today is Rememberance Day, and the town is gathering for their annual celebration. Everyone is gathering at the Memory Machine, an ingenious device that allows the townsfolk to give up their painful memories of the past to generate energy that powers the town. Lights, ovens, harvesting tools, even the blacksmith’s forge – all are powered by the Machine. Every year, the villagers supply their most painful memories and the newly unburdened town is happy and prepared for another year...

...That is, until some Greenfolk sabotaged the Memory Machine, stole some parts, and ran off into the nearby forest! This group of sentient vegetables has always been antagonistic, but this time they’ve gone too far. As the previously surrendered memories return to the townsfolk, Arthur vows to get the parts back and get the Memory Machine running again. But with their bad memories returning, he’ll also have to help them all work through a past they didn’t remember they had.

Note: Playtime is estimated to be 2-3 hours.

  • Play through an interactive story set in a mountain village, illustrated with beautiful pixel art

  • Meet a cast of memorable human and vegetable characters, brought to life with hand-drawn portraits

  • Explore themes of reconciliation and reclaiming the past

  • Listen to original music and take in the forest ambiance
